sectiondef.header: Defined BR/EDR Page Scan Intervals and Windows

briefdescription.para: Default page scan interval (R0) (0x0800, 1.280 s, 0.625 ms units).
Default page scan window (R0) (0x0800, 1.280 s, 0.625 ms units).
Fast page scan interval (R1) (0x00a0, 100.0 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Fast page scan window (R1) (0x0011, 10.625 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Medium page scan interval (R1) (0x0800, 1.280 s, 0.625 ms units).
Medium page scan window (R1) (0x0011, 10.625 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Slow page scan interval (R1) (0x0800, 1.280 s, 0.625 ms units).
Slow page scan window (R1) (0x0011, 10.625 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Fast page scan interval (R2) (0x1000, 2.560 s, 0.625 ms units).
Fast page scan window (R2) (0x0011, 10.625 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Slow page scan interval (R2) (0x1000, 2.560 s, 0.625 ms units).
Slow page scan window (R2) (0x0011, 10.625 ms, 0.625 ms units).

sectiondef.header: Defined BR Inquiry Scan Intervals and Windows

briefdescription.para: Default inquiry scan interval (0x1000, 2.560 s, 0.625 ms units).
Default inquiry scan window (0x0012, 11.25 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Convenience value when no options are specified.
Connectable advertising.

detaileddescription.para: Starting connectable advertising preallocates a connection object. If this fails, the API returns -ENOMEM. Stopping connectable advertising will free the connection object, and will trigger a call to bt_conn_cb::recycled.
The advertising set stops immediately after it creates a connection. This happens automatically in the controller.

simplesect.para: To continue advertising after a connection is created, the application should listen for the bt_conn_cb::connected event and start the advertising set again. Note that the advertiser cannot be started when all connection objects are in use. In that case, defer starting the advertiser until bt_conn_cb::recycled. To continue after a disconnection, listen for bt_conn_cb::recycled.

briefdescription.para: Advertise using identity address.

detaileddescription.para:
Advertise using the identity address as the advertiser address.
This will compromise the privacy of the device, so care must be taken when using this option.
The address used for advertising will not be the same as returned by bt_le_oob_get_local, instead bt_id_get should be used to get the LE address.

briefdescription.para: Low duty cycle directed advertising.

detaileddescription.para: Use low duty directed advertising mode, otherwise high duty mode will be used.

briefdescription.para: Directed advertising to privacy-enabled peer.

detaileddescription.para: Enable use of Resolvable Private Address (RPA) as the target address in directed advertisements. This is required if the remote device is privacy-enabled and supports address resolution of the target address in directed advertisement. It is the responsibility of the application to check that the remote device supports address resolution of directed advertisements by reading its Central Address Resolution characteristic.

briefdescription.para: Use filter accept list to filter devices that can request scan response data.
Use filter accept list to filter devices that can connect.
Notify the application when a scan response data has been sent to an active scanner.
Support scan response data.

detaileddescription.para: When used together with BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV then this option cannot be used together with the BT_LE_ADV_OPT_CONN option. When used together with BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV then scan response data must be set.

briefdescription.para: Advertise with extended advertising.

detaileddescription.para: This options enables extended advertising in the advertising set. In extended advertising the advertising set will send a small header packet on the three primary advertising channels. This small header points to the advertising data packet that will be sent on one of the 37 secondary advertising channels. The advertiser will send primary advertising on LE 1M PHY, and secondary advertising on LE 2M PHY. Connections will be established on LE 2M PHY.
Without this option the advertiser will send advertising data on the three primary advertising channels.

simplesect.para: Enabling this option requires extended advertising support in the peer devices scanning for advertisement packets.
This cannot be used with bt_le_adv_start.

briefdescription.para: Disable use of LE 2M PHY on the secondary advertising channel.

detaileddescription.para: Disabling the use of LE 2M PHY could be necessary if scanners don't support the LE 2M PHY. The advertiser will send primary advertising on LE 1M PHY, and secondary advertising on LE 1M PHY. Connections will be established on LE 1M PHY.

simplesect.para: Cannot be set if BT_LE_ADV_OPT_CODED is set.
Requires BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV bit (see bt_le_adv_opt field) to be set as bt_le_adv_param::options.

briefdescription.para: Advertise on the LE Coded PHY (Long Range).

detaileddescription.para: The advertiser will send both primary and secondary advertising on the LE Coded PHY. This gives the advertiser increased range with the trade-off of lower data rate and higher power consumption. Connections will be established on LE Coded PHY.

simplesect.para: Requires BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV bit (see bt_le_adv_opt field) to be set as bt_le_adv_param::options.

briefdescription.para: Advertise without a device address (identity address or RPA).

simplesect.para: Requires BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV bit (see bt_le_adv_opt field) to be set as bt_le_adv_param::options.

briefdescription.para: Advertise with transmit power.

simplesect.para: Requires BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV bit (see bt_le_adv_opt field) to be set as bt_le_adv_param::options.

briefdescription.para: Disable advertising on channel index 37.
Disable advertising on channel index 38.
Disable advertising on channel index 39.
Advertise using a Non-Resolvable Private Address.

detaileddescription.para: A new NRPA is set when updating the advertising parameters.
This is an advanced feature; most users will want to enable CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV instead.

simplesect.para: Mutually exclusive with BT_LE_ADV_OPT_USE_IDENTITY.

briefdescription.para: Configures the advertiser to use the S=2 coding scheme for LE Coded PHY.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the advertiser's required coding scheme to S=2, which is one of the coding options available for LE Coded PHY. The S=2 coding scheme offers higher data rates compared to S=8, with a trade-off of reduced range. The coding scheme will only be set if both the primary and secondary advertising channels indicate LE Coded Phy. Additionally, the Controller must support the LE Feature Advertising Coding Selection. If these conditions are not met, it will default to no required coding scheme.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: BT_EXT_ADV_CODING_SELECTION.

briefdescription.para: Configures the advertiser to use the S=8 coding scheme for LE Coded PHY.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the advertiser's required coding scheme to S=8, which is one of the coding options available for LE Coded PHY. The S=8 coding scheme offers increased range compared to S=2, with a trade-off of lower data rates. The coding scheme will only be set if both the primary and secondary advertising channels indicate LE Coded Phy. Additionally, the Controller must support the LE Feature Advertising Coding Selection. If these conditions are not met, it will default to no required coding scheme.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: BT_EXT_ADV_CODING_SELECTION.

briefdescription.para: Advertising options.
Convenience value when no options are specified.
Advertise with transmit power.

simplesect.para: Requires BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV bit (see bt_le_adv_opt field) to be set as bt_le_adv_param::options.

briefdescription.para: Advertise with included AdvDataInfo (ADI).

simplesect.para: Requires BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV bit (see bt_le_adv_opt field) to be set as bt_le_adv_param::options.

briefdescription.para: Periodic Advertising options.
The advertising set has been created but not enabled.
The advertising set is enabled.
Advertising states.
Not configured for periodic advertising.
The advertising set has been configured for periodic advertising, but is not enabled.
Periodic advertising is enabled.
Periodic Advertising states.
Convenience value when no options are specified.
Use the periodic advertising list to sync with advertiser.

detaileddescription.para: When this option is set, the address and SID of the parameters are ignored.

briefdescription.para: Disables periodic advertising reports.

detaileddescription.para: No advertisement reports will be handled until enabled.

briefdescription.para: Filter duplicate Periodic Advertising reports.
Sync with Angle of Arrival (AoA) constant tone extension.
Sync with Angle of Departure (AoD) 1 us constant tone extension.
Sync with Angle of Departure (AoD) 2 us constant tone extension.
Do not sync to packets without a constant tone extension.
Periodic advertising sync options.
Convenience value when no options are specified.
No Angle of Arrival (AoA)

detaileddescription.para: Do not sync with Angle of Arrival (AoA) constant tone extension

briefdescription.para: No Angle of Departure (AoD) 1 us.

detaileddescription.para: Do not sync with Angle of Departure (AoD) 1 us constant tone extension

briefdescription.para: No Angle of Departure (AoD) 2.

detaileddescription.para: Do not sync with Angle of Departure (AoD) 2 us constant tone extension

briefdescription.para: Only sync to packets with constant tone extension.
Sync to received PAST packets but don't generate sync reports.

detaileddescription.para: This option must not be set at the same time as BT_LE_PER_ADV_SYNC_TRANSFER_OPT_FILTER_DUPLICATES.

briefdescription.para: Sync to received PAST packets and generate sync reports with duplicate filtering.

detaileddescription.para: This option must not be set at the same time as BT_LE_PER_ADV_SYNC_TRANSFER_OPT_REPORTING_INITIALLY_DISABLED.

briefdescription.para: Periodic Advertising Sync Transfer options.
Convenience value when no options are specified.
Filter duplicates.
Filter using filter accept list.
Enable scan on coded PHY (Long Range).
Disable scan on 1M phy.

simplesect.para: Requires BT_LE_SCAN_OPT_CODED.

briefdescription.para: Scan without requesting additional information from advertisers.
Scan and request additional information from advertisers.

detaileddescription.para: Using this scan type will automatically send scan requests to all devices. Scan responses are received in the same manner and using the same callbacks as advertising reports.

briefdescription.para: Accept connection as central role.
Accept connection as peripheral role.
Reject connection due to insufficient resources.
Reject connection due to insufficient security.
Reject connection due to unacceptable device address.
BR/EDR connection request response codes.

detaileddescription.para: Response codes for incoming BR/EDR connection requests, used by the connection request callback to accept or reject connections.

briefdescription.para: Standard scan (default)
Interlaced scan (1.2 devices only)
Page scan type.
Callback for notifying that Bluetooth has been enabled.

parameterdescription.para: zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const bt_addr_le_t *addr, int8_t rssi, uint8_t adv_type, struct net_buf_simple *buf)
memberdef.name: bt_le_scan_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Callback type for reporting LE scan results.

detaileddescription.para: A function of this type is given to the bt_le_scan_start function and will be called for any discovered LE device.

parameterdescription.para: Type of advertising response from advertiser. Uses the bt_gap_adv_type values.

memberdef.definition: typedef enum bt_br_conn_req_rsp(* bt_br_conn_req_func_t) (const bt_addr_t *addr, uint32_t cod)
memberdef.name: bt_br_conn_req_func_t

briefdescription.para: Callback type for incoming BR/EDR connection requests.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is invoked when a remote Bluetooth Classic device attempts to establish a connection to the local controller. The application must return a response code to accept or reject the connection, and specify the desired role if accepting.

parameterdescription.para: Remote device address attempting to connect.
Class of Device of the remote device.

simplesect.para: bt_br_conn_req_rsp Response code indicating whether to accept or reject the connection, and the desired role if accepting.

briefdescription.para: Enable Bluetooth.

detaileddescription.para: Enable Bluetooth. Must be the called before any calls that require communication with the local Bluetooth hardware.

When CONFIG_BT_SETTINGS is enabled, the application must load the Bluetooth settings after this API call successfully completes before Bluetooth APIs can be used. Loading the settings before calling this function is insufficient. Bluetooth settings can be loaded with settings_load or settings_load_subtree with argument "bt". The latter selectively loads only Bluetooth settings and is recommended if settings_load has been called earlier.

parameterdescription.para: Callback to notify completion or NULL to perform the enabling synchronously. The callback is called from the system workqueue.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Disable Bluetooth.

detaileddescription.para: Disable Bluetooth. Can't be called before bt_enable has completed.
This API will clear all configured identity addresses and keys that are not persistently stored with CONFIG_BT_SETTINGS. These can be restored with settings_load before reenabling the stack.

This API does not clear previously registered callbacks like bt_le_scan_cb_register, bt_conn_cb_register AND bt_br_discovery_cb_register. That is, the application shall not re-register them when the Bluetooth subsystem is re-enabled later.
Close and release HCI resources. Result is architecture dependent.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if Bluetooth is ready.

simplesect.para: true when Bluetooth is ready, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Set Bluetooth Device Name.

detaileddescription.para: Set Bluetooth GAP Device Name.

simplesect.para: The advertising data is not automatically updated. When advertising with device name in the advertising data, the name should be updated by calling bt_le_adv_update_data or bt_le_ext_adv_set_data after the call to this function.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_DEVICE_NAME_DYNAMIC.
CONFIG_BT_DEVICE_NAME_MAX.

parameterdescription.para: New name, must be null terminated

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get Bluetooth Device Name.

detaileddescription.para: Get Bluetooth GAP Device Name.

simplesect.para: Bluetooth Device Name

briefdescription.para: Get local Bluetooth appearance.

detaileddescription.para: Bluetooth Appearance is a description of the external appearance of a device in terms of an Appearance Value.

simplesect.para: Section 2.6 of the Bluetooth SIG Assigned Numbers document.
Appearance Value of local Bluetooth host.

briefdescription.para: Set local Bluetooth appearance.

detaileddescription.para: Automatically preserves the new appearance across reboots if CONFIG_BT_SETTINGS is enabled.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_DEVICE_APPEARANCE_DYNAMIC.

parameterdescription.para: Persistent storage failed. Appearance was not updated.

briefdescription.para: Get the currently configured identity addresses.

detaileddescription.para: Returns an array of the currently configured identity addresses. To make sure all available identity addresses can be retrieved, the number of elements in the addrs array should be CONFIG_BT_ID_MAX. The identity handle that some APIs expect (such as bt_le_adv_param) is simply the index of the identity address in the addrs array.
If addrs is passed as NULL, then the returned count contains the count of all available identity addresses that can be retrieved with a subsequent call to this function with non-NULL addrs parameter.

simplesect.para: Deleted identity addresses may show up as BT_ADDR_LE_ANY in the returned array.

parameterdescription.para: Array where to store the configured identity addresses.
Should be initialized to the array size. Once the function returns it will contain the number of returned identity addresses.

briefdescription.para: Create a new identity address.

detaileddescription.para: Create a new identity address using the given address and IRK. This function can be called before calling bt_enable. However, the new identity address will only be stored persistently in flash when this API is used after bt_enable. The reason is that the persistent settings are loaded after bt_enable and would therefore cause potential conflicts with the stack blindly overwriting what's stored in flash. The identity address will also not be written to flash in case a pre-defined address is provided, since in such a situation the app clearly has some place it got the address from and will be able to repeat the procedure on every power cycle, i.e. it would be redundant to also store the information in flash.
Generating random static address or random IRK is not supported when calling this function before bt_enable.

If the application wants to have the stack randomly generate identity addresses and store them in flash for later recovery, the way to do it would be to first initialize the stack (using bt_enable), then call settings_load, and after that check with bt_id_get how many identity addresses were recovered. If an insufficient amount of identity addresses were recovered the app may then call this function to create new ones.

simplesect.para: If CONFIG_BT_HCI_SET_PUBLIC_ADDR is enabled, the first call can set a public address as the controller's identity, but only before bt_enable and if no other identities exist.

parameterdescription.para: Address to use for the new identity address. If NULL or initialized to BT_ADDR_LE_ANY the stack will generate a new random static address for the identity address and copy it to the given parameter upon return from this function (in case the parameter was non-NULL).

Identity Resolving Key (16 octets) to be used with this identity address. If set to all zeroes or NULL, the stack will generate a random IRK for the identity address and copy it back to the parameter upon return from this function (in case the parameter was non-NULL). If privacy CONFIG_BT_PRIVACY is not enabled this parameter must be NULL.

simplesect.para: Identity handle (>= 0) in case of success, or a negative error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Reset/reclaim an identity address for reuse.

detaileddescription.para: When given an existing identity handle, this function will disconnect any connections (to the corresponding identity address) created using it, remove any pairing keys or other data associated with it, and then create a new identity address in the same slot, based on the addr and irk parameters.

simplesect.para: The default identity address (corresponding to BT_ID_DEFAULT) cannot be reset, and this API will return an error if asked to do that.

parameterdescription.para: Address to use for the new identity address. If NULL or initialized to BT_ADDR_LE_ANY the stack will generate a new static random address for the identity address and copy it to the given parameter upon return from this function.

Identity Resolving Key (16 octets) to be used with this identity address. If set to all zeroes or NULL, the stack will generate a random IRK for the identity address and copy it back to the parameter upon return from this function (in case the parameter was non-NULL). If privacy CONFIG_BT_PRIVACY is not enabled this parameter must be NULL.

simplesect.para: Identity handle (>= 0) in case of success, or a negative error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Delete an identity address.

detaileddescription.para: When given a valid identity handle this function will disconnect any connections (to the corresponding identity address) created using it, remove any pairing keys or other data associated with it, and then flag is as deleted, so that it can not be used for any operations. To take back into use the slot the identity address was occupying, the bt_id_reset API needs to be used.

simplesect.para: The default identity address (corresponding to BT_ID_DEFAULT) cannot be deleted, and this API will return an error if asked to do that.
0 in case of success, or a negative error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the total size (in octets) of a given set of bt_data structures.

detaileddescription.para: The total size includes the length (1 octet) and type (1 octet) fields for each element, plus their respective data lengths.

parameterdescription.para: Number of bt_data structures in data.

simplesect.para: Size of the concatenated data, built from the bt_data structure set.

briefdescription.para: Serialize a bt_data struct into an advertising structure (a flat array).

detaileddescription.para: The data are formatted according to the Bluetooth Core Specification v. 5.4, vol. 3, part C, 11.

parameterdescription.para: Single bt_data structure to read from.
Buffer large enough to store the advertising structure in input. The size of it must be at least the size of the input->data_len + 2 (for the type and the length).

simplesect.para: Number of octets written in output.

briefdescription.para: Get local Bluetooth LE controller features.

detaileddescription.para: Can only be called after bt_enable.

parameterdescription.para: Local features struct to be populated with information.
The information is not yet available.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_le_adv_param *param, const struct bt_data *ad, size_t ad_len, const struct bt_data *sd, size_t sd_len)
memberdef.name: bt_le_adv_start

briefdescription.para: Start advertising.

detaileddescription.para: Set advertisement data, scan response data, advertisement parameters and start advertising.
When param.peer is set, the advertising will be directed to that peer device. In this case, the other function parameters are ignored.
This function cannot be used with BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV in the param.options. For extended advertising, the bt_le_ext_adv_* functions must be used.

parameterdescription.para: Data to be used in advertisement packets.
Data to be used in scan response packets.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.
-ENOMEM No free connection objects available for connectable advertiser.

-ECONNREFUSED When connectable advertising is requested and there is already maximum number of connections established in the controller. This error code is only guaranteed when using Zephyr controller, for other controllers code returned in this case may be -EIO.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_data *ad, size_t ad_len, const struct bt_data *sd, size_t sd_len)
memberdef.name: bt_le_adv_update_data

briefdescription.para: Update advertising.

detaileddescription.para: Update advertisement and scan response data.

parameterdescription.para: Data to be used in advertisement packets.
Data to be used in scan response packets.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Stop advertising.

detaileddescription.para: Stops ongoing advertising.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_le_adv_param *param, const struct bt_le_ext_adv_cb *cb, struct bt_le_ext_adv **adv)
memberdef.name: bt_le_ext_adv_create

briefdescription.para: Create advertising set.

detaileddescription.para: Create an instance of an independent advertising set with its own parameters and data. The advertising set remains valid until deleted with bt_le_ext_adv_delete. Advertising parameters can be updated with bt_le_ext_adv_update_param, and advertising can be started with bt_le_ext_adv_start.

simplesect.para: The number of supported extended advertising sets can be controlled by CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV_MAX_ADV_SET.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct to notify about advertiser activity. Can be NULL. Must point to valid memory during the lifetime of the advertising set.
Valid advertising set object on success.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Start advertising with the given advertising set.

detaileddescription.para: If the advertiser is limited by either the param.timeout or param.num_events, the application will be notified by the bt_le_ext_adv_cb::sent callback once the limit is reached. If the advertiser is limited by both the timeout and the number of advertising events, then the limit that is reached first will stop the advertiser.

simplesect.para: The advertising set adv can be created with bt_le_ext_adv_create.

briefdescription.para: Stop advertising with the given advertising set.

detaileddescription.para: Stop advertising with a specific advertising set. When using this function the advertising sent callback will not be called.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_le_ext_adv *adv, const struct bt_data *ad, size_t ad_len, const struct bt_data *sd, size_t sd_len)
memberdef.name: bt_le_ext_adv_set_data

briefdescription.para: Set an advertising set's advertising or scan response data.

detaileddescription.para: Set advertisement data or scan response data. If the advertising set is currently advertising then the advertising data will be updated in subsequent advertising events.

When both BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV and BT_LE_ADV_OPT_SCANNABLE are enabled then advertising data is ignored and only scan response data is used. When BT_LE_ADV_OPT_SCANNABLE is not enabled then scan response data is ignored and only advertising data is used.

If the advertising set has been configured to send advertising data on the primary advertising channels then the maximum data length is BT_GAP_ADV_MAX_ADV_DATA_LEN octets. If the advertising set has been configured for extended advertising, then the maximum data length is defined by the controller with the maximum possible of BT_GAP_ADV_MAX_EXT_ADV_DATA_LEN bytes.

simplesect.para: Extended advertising was introduced in Bluetooth 5.0, and legacy scanners will not support reception of any extended advertising packets.

When updating the advertising data while advertising the advertising data and scan response data length must be smaller or equal to what can be fit in a single advertising packet. Otherwise the advertiser must be stopped.

parameterdescription.para: Data to be used in advertisement packets.
Data to be used in scan response packets.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Update advertising parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Update the advertising parameters. The function will return an error if the advertiser set is currently advertising. Stop the advertising set before calling this function.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Delete advertising set.

detaileddescription.para: Delete advertising set. This will free up the advertising set and make it possible to create a new advertising set if the limit CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV_MAX_ADV_SET was reached.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get array index of an advertising set.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to map bt_adv to index of an array of advertising sets. The array has CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV_MAX_ADV_SET elements.

simplesect.para: Index of the advertising set object. The range of the returned value is 0..CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV_MAX_ADV_SET-1

briefdescription.para: Get advertising set info.

parameterdescription.para: Advertising set info object. The values in this object are only valid on success.
adv is not valid advertising set or info is NULL.

briefdescription.para: Set or update the periodic advertising parameters.

detaileddescription.para: The periodic advertising parameters can only be set or updated on an extended advertisement set which is neither scannable, connectable nor anonymous (meaning, the advertising options BT_LE_ADV_OPT_SCANNABLE, BT_LE_ADV_OPT_CONN and BT_LE_ADV_OPT_ANONYMOUS cannot be set for adv).

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_le_ext_adv *adv, const struct bt_data *ad, size_t ad_len)
memberdef.name: bt_le_per_adv_set_data

briefdescription.para: Set or update the periodic advertising data.

detaileddescription.para: The periodic advertisement data can only be set or updated on an extended advertisement set which is neither scannable, connectable nor anonymous (meaning, the advertising options BT_LE_ADV_OPT_SCANNABLE, BT_LE_ADV_OPT_CONN and BT_LE_ADV_OPT_ANONYMOUS cannot be set for adv).

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_le_ext_adv *adv, uint8_t num_subevents, const struct bt_le_per_adv_subevent_data_params *params)
memberdef.name: bt_le_per_adv_set_subevent_data

briefdescription.para: Set the periodic advertising with response subevent data.

detaileddescription.para: Set the data for one or more subevents of a Periodic Advertising with Responses Advertiser in reply data request.

simplesect.para: There are num_subevents elements in params.
The controller has requested data for the subevents in params.

parameterdescription.para: The extended advertiser the PAwR train belongs to.
The number of subevents to set data for.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Starts periodic advertising.

detaileddescription.para: Enabling the periodic advertising can be done independently of extended advertising, but both periodic advertising and extended advertising shall be enabled before any periodic advertising data is sent. The periodic advertising and extended advertising can be enabled in any order.

Once periodic advertising has been enabled, it will continue advertising until bt_le_per_adv_stop function has been called, or if the advertising set is deleted by bt_le_ext_adv_delete function. Calling bt_le_ext_adv_stop function will not stop the periodic advertising.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Stops periodic advertising.

detaileddescription.para: Disabling the periodic advertising can be done independently of extended advertising. Disabling periodic advertising will not disable extended advertising.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get array index of an periodic advertising sync object.

detaileddescription.para: This function is to get the index of an array of periodic advertising sync objects. The array has CONFIG_BT_PER_ADV_SYNC_MAX elements.

simplesect.para: Index of the periodic advertising sync object. The range of the returned value is 0..CONFIG_BT_PER_ADV_SYNC_MAX-1

briefdescription.para: Get a periodic advertising sync object from the array index.

detaileddescription.para: This function is to get the periodic advertising sync object from the array index. The array has CONFIG_BT_PER_ADV_SYNC_MAX elements.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the periodic advertising sync object. The range of the index value is 0..CONFIG_BT_PER_ADV_SYNC_MAX-1

simplesect.para: The periodic advertising sync object of the array index or NULL if invalid index.

briefdescription.para: Get periodic adv sync information.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Look up an existing periodic advertising sync object by advertiser address.

simplesect.para: Periodic advertising sync object or NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Create a periodic advertising sync object.

detaileddescription.para: Create a periodic advertising sync object that can try to synchronize to periodic advertising reports from an advertiser. Scan shall either be disabled or extended scan shall be enabled.

This function does not timeout, and will continue to look for an advertiser until it either finds it or bt_le_per_adv_sync_delete is called. It is thus suggested to implement a timeout when using this, if it is expected to find the advertiser within a reasonable timeframe.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Delete periodic advertising sync.

detaileddescription.para: Delete the periodic advertising sync object. Can be called regardless of the state of the sync. If the syncing is currently syncing, the syncing is cancelled. If the sync has been established, it is terminated. The periodic advertising sync object will be invalidated afterwards.
If the state of the sync object is syncing, then a new periodic advertising sync object cannot be created until the controller has finished canceling this object.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Register periodic advertising sync callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: Adds the callback structure to the list of callback structures for periodic advertising syncs.
This callback will be called for all periodic advertising sync activity, such as synced, terminated and when data is received.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct. Must point to memory that remains valid.

briefdescription.para: Unregister periodic advertising sync callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: Removes the callback structure from the list of periodic advertising sync callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Enables receiving periodic advertising reports for a sync.

detaileddescription.para: If the sync is already receiving the reports, -EALREADY is returned.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Disables receiving periodic advertising reports for a sync.

detaileddescription.para: If the sync report receiving is already disabled, -EALREADY is returned.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_le_per_adv_sync *per_adv_sync, const struct bt_conn *conn, uint16_t service_data)
memberdef.name: bt_le_per_adv_sync_transfer

briefdescription.para: Transfer the periodic advertising sync information to a peer device.

detaileddescription.para: This will allow another device to quickly synchronize to the same periodic advertising train that this device is currently synced to.

parameterdescription.para: The periodic advertising sync to transfer.
The peer device that will receive the sync information.
Application service data provided to the remote host.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_le_ext_adv *adv, const struct bt_conn *conn, uint16_t service_data)
memberdef.name: bt_le_per_adv_set_info_transfer

briefdescription.para: Transfer the information about a periodic advertising set.

detaileddescription.para: This will allow another device to quickly synchronize to periodic advertising set from this device.

parameterdescription.para: The periodic advertising set to transfer info of.
The peer device that will receive the information.
Application service data provided to the remote host.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Subscribe to periodic advertising sync transfers (PASTs).

detaileddescription.para: Sets the parameters and allow other devices to transfer periodic advertising syncs.

parameterdescription.para: The connection to set the parameters for. If NULL default parameters for all connections will be set. Parameters set for specific connection will always have precedence.
The periodic advertising sync transfer parameters.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Unsubscribe from periodic advertising sync transfers (PASTs).

detaileddescription.para: Remove the parameters that allow other devices to transfer periodic advertising syncs.

parameterdescription.para: The connection to remove the parameters for. If NULL default parameters for all connections will be removed. Unsubscribing for a specific device, will still allow other devices to transfer periodic advertising syncs.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Add a device to the periodic advertising list.

detaileddescription.para: Add peer device LE address to the periodic advertising list. This will make it possibly to automatically create a periodic advertising sync to this device.

parameterdescription.para: The advertising set ID. This value is obtained from the bt_le_scan_recv_info in the scan callback.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Remove a device from the periodic advertising list.

detaileddescription.para: Removes peer device LE address from the periodic advertising list.

parameterdescription.para: The advertising set ID. This value is obtained from the bt_le_scan_recv_info in the scan callback.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Clear the periodic advertising list.

detaileddescription.para: Clears the entire periodic advertising list.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Start (LE) scanning.

detaileddescription.para: Start LE scanning with given parameters and provide results through the specified callback.

simplesect.para: The LE scanner by default does not use the Identity Address of the local device when CONFIG_BT_PRIVACY is disabled. This is to prevent the active scanner from disclosing the identity address information when requesting additional information from advertisers. In order to enable directed advertiser reports then CONFIG_BT_SCAN_WITH_IDENTITY must be enabled.

Setting the param.timeout parameter is not supported when CONFIG_BT_PRIVACY is enabled, when the param.type is BT_LE_SCAN_TYPE_ACTIVE. Supplying a non-zero timeout will result in an -EINVAL error code.
The scanner will automatically scan for extended advertising packets if their support is enabled through CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV.

parameterdescription.para: Callback to notify scan results. May be NULL if callback registration through bt_le_scan_cb_register is preferred.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

parameterdescription.para: if the scanner is already being started in a different thread.

briefdescription.para: Stop (LE) scanning.

detaileddescription.para: Stops ongoing LE scanning.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Register scanner packet callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: Adds the callback structure to the list of callback structures that monitors scanner activity.
This callback will be called for all scanner activity.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct. Must point to memory that remains valid.

briefdescription.para: Unregister scanner packet callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: Remove the callback structure from the list of scanner callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct. Must point to memory that remains valid.

briefdescription.para: Add device (LE) to filter accept list.

detaileddescription.para: Add peer device LE address to the filter accept list.

simplesect.para: The filter accept list cannot be modified when an LE role is using the filter accept list, i.e advertiser or scanner using a filter accept list or automatic connecting to devices using filter accept list.
Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Remove device (LE) from filter accept list.

detaileddescription.para: Remove peer device LE address from the filter accept list.

simplesect.para: The filter accept list cannot be modified when an LE role is using the filter accept list, i.e advertiser or scanner using a filter accept list or automatic connecting to devices using filter accept list.
Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Clear filter accept list.

detaileddescription.para: Clear all devices from the filter accept list.

simplesect.para: The filter accept list cannot be modified when an LE role is using the filter accept list, i.e advertiser or scanner using a filter accept list or automatic connecting to devices using filter accept list.
Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Set (LE) channel map.

detaileddescription.para: Used to inform the Controller of known channel classifications. The Host can specify which channels are bad or unknown by setting the corresponding bit in the channel map to respectively 0 or 1.

simplesect.para: The interval between two succesive calls to this function must be at least one second.

parameterdescription.para: Channel map. 5 octets where each bit represents a channel. Only the lower 37 bits are valid.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Set the Resolvable Private Address timeout in runtime.

detaileddescription.para: The new RPA timeout value will be used for the next RPA rotation and all subsequent rotations until another override is scheduled with this API.
Initially, CONFIG_BT_RPA_TIMEOUT is used as the RPA timeout.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_RPA_TIMEOUT_DYNAMIC..

parameterdescription.para: Resolvable Private Address timeout in seconds.
RPA timeout value is invalid. Valid range is 1s - 3600s.

briefdescription.para: Helper for parsing advertising (or EIR or OOB) data.

detaileddescription.para: A helper for parsing the basic AD Types used for Extended Inquiry Response (EIR), Advertising Data (AD), and OOB data blocks. The most common scenario is to call this helper on the advertising data received in the callback that was given to bt_le_scan_start.

simplesect.para: This helper function will consume ad when parsing. The user should make a copy if the original data is to be used afterwards. This can be done by using net_buf_simple_save to store the state prior to the function call, and then using net_buf_simple_restore to restore the state afterwards.

parameterdescription.para: Advertising data as given to the bt_le_scan_cb_t callback.
Callback function which will be called for each element that's found in the data. The callback should return true to continue parsing, or false to stop parsing.
User data to be passed to the callback.

briefdescription.para: Get local LE Out of Band (OOB) information.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to get local information that are useful for Out of Band pairing or connection creation.

If privacy CONFIG_BT_PRIVACY is enabled this will result in generating new Resolvable Private Address (RPA) that is valid for CONFIG_BT_RPA_TIMEOUT seconds. This address will be used for advertising started by bt_le_adv_start, active scanning and connection creation.

simplesect.para:
If privacy is enabled the RPA cannot be refreshed in the following cases:
Creating a connection in progress, wait for the connected callback. In addition when extended advertising CONFIG_BT_EXT_ADV is not enabled or not supported by the controller:
Advertiser is enabled using a Random Static Identity Address as a different local identity address.
The local identity address conflicts with the local identity address used by other roles.
This function randomly generates cryptographic material used in the pairing process, and should be called again for each new pairing process.

parameterdescription.para: Local identity handle (typically BT_ID_DEFAULT). Corresponds to the identity address this function will be called for.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Get local LE Out of Band (OOB) information.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to get local information that are useful for Out of Band pairing or connection creation.

If privacy CONFIG_BT_PRIVACY is enabled this will result in generating new Resolvable Private Address (RPA) that is valid for CONFIG_BT_RPA_TIMEOUT seconds. This address will be used by the advertising set.

simplesect.para: When generating OOB information for multiple advertising set all OOB information needs to be generated at the same time.

If privacy is enabled the RPA cannot be refreshed in the following cases:
Creating a connection in progress, wait for the connected callback.
This function randomly generates cryptographic material used in the pairing process, and should be called again for each new pairing process.
Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Clear pairing information.

parameterdescription.para: Local identity handle (typically BT_ID_DEFAULT). Corresponds to the identity address this function will be called for.
Remote address, NULL or BT_ADDR_LE_ANY to clear all remote devices.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint8_t id, void(*func)(const struct bt_bond_info *info, void *user_data), void *user_data)
memberdef.name: bt_foreach_bond

briefdescription.para: Iterate through all existing bonds.

parameterdescription.para: Local identity handle (typically BT_ID_DEFAULT). Corresponds to the identity address used in iteration.
Function to call for each bond.
Data to pass to the callback function.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint8_t dir, uint8_t id, uint8_t vs_config_len, const uint8_t *vs_config)
memberdef.name: bt_configure_data_path

briefdescription.para: Configure vendor data path.

detaileddescription.para: Request the Controller to configure the data transport path in a given direction between the Controller and the Host.

parameterdescription.para: Direction to be configured, BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_HOST_TO_CTLR or BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_CTLR_TO_HOST
Vendor specific logical transport channel ID, range [BT_HCI_DATAPATH_ID_VS..BT_HCI_DATAPATH_ID_VS_END]
Length of additional vendor specific configuration data
Pointer to additional vendor specific configuration data

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Synchronize with a subset of subevents.

detaileddescription.para: Until this command is issued, the subevent(s) the controller is synchronized to is unspecified.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_le_per_adv_sync *per_adv_sync, const struct bt_le_per_adv_response_params *params, const struct net_buf_simple *data)
memberdef.name: bt_le_per_adv_set_response_data

briefdescription.para: Set the data for a response slot in a specific subevent of the PAwR.

detaileddescription.para: This function is called by the application to set the response data. The data for a response slot shall be transmitted only once.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if a device identified by a Bluetooth LE address is bonded.

detaileddescription.para: Valid Bluetooth LE identity addresses are either public address or random static address.

parameterdescription.para: Local identity handle (typically BT_ID_DEFAULT). Corresponds to the identity address this function will be called for.

simplesect.para: true if addr is bonded with local id

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_br_discovery_param *param, struct bt_br_discovery_result *results, size_t count)
memberdef.name: bt_br_discovery_start

briefdescription.para: Start BR/EDR discovery.

detaileddescription.para: Start BR/EDR discovery (inquiry) and provide results through the specified callback. The discovery results will be notified through callbacks registered by bt_br_discovery_cb_register. If more inquiry results were received during session than fits in provided result storage, only ones with highest RSSI will be reported.

parameterdescription.para: Number of results in storage. Valid range: 1-255.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error

briefdescription.para: Stop BR/EDR discovery.

detaileddescription.para: Stops ongoing BR/EDR discovery. If discovery was stopped by this call results won't be reported

simplesect.para: Zero on success or error code otherwise, positive in case of protocol error or negative (POSIX) in case of stack internal error.

briefdescription.para: Register discovery packet callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: Adds the callback structure to the list of callback structures that monitors inquiry activity.
This callback will be called for all inquiry activity, regardless of what API was used to start the discovery.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct. Must point to memory that remains valid.

briefdescription.para: Unregister discovery packet callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: Remove the callback structure from the list of discovery callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: Callback struct. Must point to memory that remains valid.

briefdescription.para: Get BR/EDR local Out Of Band information.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to get local controller information that are useful for Out Of Band pairing or connection creation process.

briefdescription.para: Enable/disable set controller in discoverable state.

detaileddescription.para: Allows make local controller to listen on INQUIRY SCAN channel and responds to devices making general inquiry. To enable this state it's mandatory to first be in connectable state.
If the device enters limited discoverable mode, the controller will leave from discoverable mode after the duration of BT_LIMITED_DISCOVERABLE_DURATION seconds in the limited discoverable mode.

parameterdescription.para: Value allowing/disallowing controller to become discoverable.
Value allowing/disallowing controller to enter limited discoverable mode.

simplesect.para: Negative if fail set to requested state or requested state has been already set. Zero if done successfully.

briefdescription.para: Enable/disable set controller in connectable state.

detaileddescription.para: Allows make local controller to be connectable. It means the controller start listen to devices requests on PAGE SCAN channel. If disabled also resets discoverability if was set.

parameterdescription.para: Value allowing/disallowing controller to be connectable.

Callback function to handle incoming connection requests. If the enable is false, this parameter is ignored. If func is NULL, the conn_req will be accepted internally. The default role is peripheral. The role switch request can be performed if the BT_ACCEPT_CONN_AS_CENTRAL is enabled. If func is provided, the conn_req will be passed to the callback function for the application to decide whether to accept or reject the connection, and the desired role for the connection.

simplesect.para: Negative if fail set to requested state or requested state has been already set. Zero if done successfully.

briefdescription.para: Update BR/EDR page scan parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This function updates the page scan parameters of the local BR/EDR controller. Page scan parameters determine how the controller listens for incoming connection requests from remote devices.
The function validates the provided parameters, including the interval, window, and scan type, and sends the appropriate HCI commands to update the controller's page scan activity and scan type.

The user can set custom page scan parameters using the helper macro BT_BR_PAGE_SCAN_PARAM() to define their own values. Alternatively, the user can use predefined standard parameters as defined in the Bluetooth specification:
BT_BR_PAGE_SCAN_PARAM_R0 Default page scan parameters.
BT_BR_PAGE_SCAN_PARAM_FAST_R1 Fast page scan parameters for R1.
BT_BR_PAGE_SCAN_PARAM_MEDIUM_R1 Medium page scan parameters for R1.
BT_BR_PAGE_SCAN_PARAM_SLOW_R1 Slow page scan parameters for R1.
BT_BR_PAGE_SCAN_PARAM_FAST_R2 Fast page scan parameters for R2.
BT_BR_PAGE_SCAN_PARAM_SLOW_R2 Slow page scan parameters for R2.
These predefined parameters are designed to meet common use cases and ensure compliance with the Bluetooth specification.

parameterdescription.para:
Page scan parameters, including:
interval: Time between consecutive page scans (in 0.625 ms units). Must be in the range [0x0012, 0x1000].
window: Duration of a single page scan (in 0.625 ms units). Must be in the range [0x0011, 0x1000].
type: Page scan type (e.g., standard or interlaced).

simplesect.para: 0 on success.
-EINVAL if the provided parameters are invalid.
-EAGAIN if the device is not ready.
-ENOBUFS if memory allocation for HCI commands fails.
Other negative error codes for internal failures.

briefdescription.para: Update BR/EDR inquiry scan parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This function updates the inquiry scan parameters of the local BR/EDR controller. Inquiry scan parameters determine how the controller handles inquiry requests.
The function validates the provided parameters, including the interval, window, and scan type, and sends the appropriate HCI commands to update the controller's inquiry scan activity and scan type.

The user can set custom inquiry scan parameters using the helper macro BT_BR_INQUIRY_SCAN_PARAM to define their own values. Alternatively, the user can use predefined standard parameters as defined in the Bluetooth specification:
BT_BR_INQUIRY_SCAN_PARAM_DEFAULT Default inquiry scan parameters. These predefined parameters are designed to meet common use cases and ensure compliance with the Bluetooth specification.

parameterdescription.para:
Inquiry scan parameters, including:
interval: Time between consecutive inquiry scans (in 0.625 ms units). Must be in the range [0x0012, 0x1000].
window: Duration of a single inquiry scan (in 0.625 ms units). Must be in the range [0x0011, 0x1000].
type: Inquiry scan type (e.g., standard or interlaced).

simplesect.para: 0 on success.
-EINVAL if the provided parameters are invalid.
-EAGAIN if the device is not ready.
-ENOBUFS if memory allocation for HCI commands fails.
Other negative error codes for internal failures.

briefdescription.para: Set the Class of Device of the local BR/EDR Controller.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes the Class of Device (COD) value to the BR/EDR controller. The COD is used by remote devices during the discovery process to identify the type of device and the services it provides.

simplesect.para: The limited discoverable mode bit (BT_COD_MAJOR_SVC_CLASS_LIMITED_DISCOVER) cannot be set directly through this function. Use bt_br_set_discoverable() with the limited parameter to enable limited discoverable mode, which will automatically set the corresponding COD bit.

parameterdescription.para: Class of Device value to set. This should be a combination of Major Service Class bits (BT_COD_MAJOR_SVC_CLASS_*), Major Device Class bits (BT_COD_MAJOR_DEVICE_CLASS_*), and Minor Device Class bits (BT_COD_MINOR_DEVICE_CLASS_*).
if the Bluetooth device is not ready
if the provided COD value attempts to set the limited discoverable bit directly, which is not permitted
negative error codes from underlying HCI command failures

briefdescription.para: Get the Class of Device of the local BR/EDR Controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to where the current Class of Device value will be stored.
if the Bluetooth device is not ready

briefdescription.para: Check if a Bluetooth classic device address is bonded.

simplesect.para: true if addr is bonded

briefdescription.para: Clear classic pairing information .

parameterdescription.para: Remote address, NULL or BT_ADDR_ANY to clear all remote devices.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Iterate through all existing bonds of Classic.

parameterdescription.para: Function to call for each bond.
Data to pass to the callback function.

briefdescription.para: Identity handle referring to the first identity address.

detaileddescription.para: This is a convenience macro for specifying the default identity address. This helps make the code more readable, especially when only one identity address is supported.

briefdescription.para: Number of octets for local supported features.

detaileddescription.para: The value of 8 correspond to page 0 in the LE Controller supported features. 24 bytes are required for all subsequent supported feature pages.

briefdescription.para: Bluetooth data serialized size.

detaileddescription.para: Get the size of a serialized bt_data given its data length.
Size of 'AD Structure'->'Length' field, equal to 1. Size of 'AD Structure'->'Data'->'AD Type' field, equal to 1. Size of 'AD Structure'->'Data'->'AD Data' field, equal to data_len.
See Core Specification Version 5.4 Vol. 3 Part C, 11, Figure 11.1.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare elements of bt_data arrays.

detaileddescription.para: This macro is mainly for creating an array of struct bt_data elements which is then passed to e.g. bt_le_adv_start function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the data field payload
Number of octets behind the _data pointer

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare elements of bt_data arrays.

detaileddescription.para: This macro is mainly for creating an array of struct bt_data elements which is then passed to e.g. bt_le_adv_start function.

briefdescription.para: Initialize advertising parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Peer address, set to NULL for undirected advertising or address of peer for directed advertising.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare advertising parameters inline.

parameterdescription.para: Peer address, set to NULL for undirected advertising or address of peer for directed advertising.

briefdescription.para: GAP recommended connectable advertising parameters user-initiated.

detaileddescription.para: This define sets the recommended default for when an application is likely waiting for the device to be connected or discovered.

GAP recommends advertisers use the advertising parameters set by BT_LE_ADV_CONN_FAST_1 for user-initiated advertisements. This might mean any time a user interacts with a device, a press on a dedicated Bluetooth wakeup button, or anything in-between. Interpretation is left to the application developer.

Following modes are considered in these parameter settings:
Undirected Connectable Mode
Limited Discoverable Mode and sending connectable undirected advertising events
General Discoverable Mode and sending connectable undirected advertising events
Directed Connectable Mode and sending low duty cycle directed advertising events

These parameters are merely a recommendation. For example the application might use a longer interval to conserve battery, which would be at the cost of responsiveness and it should be considered to enter a lower power state with longer intervals only after a timeout.
This is the recommended setting for limited discoverable mode.
See Bluetooth Core Specification:
6.0 Vol 3, Part C, Appendix A "Timers and Constants", T_GAP(adv_fast_interval1)
6.0 Vol 3, Part C, Section 9.3.11 "Connection Establishment Timing parameters"

briefdescription.para: GAP recommended connectable advertising parameters non-connectable advertising events.

detaileddescription.para: This define sets the recommended default for user-initiated advertisements or sending non-connectable advertising events.

Following modes are considered in these parameter settings:
Non-Discoverable Mode
Non-Connectable Mode
Limited Discoverable Mode
General Discoverable Mode

See Bluetooth Core Specification:
6.0 Vol 3, Part C, Appendix A "Timers and Constants", T_GAP(adv_fast_interval2)
6.0 Vol 3, Part C, Section 9.3.11 "Connection Establishment Timing parameters"

briefdescription.para: Non-connectable advertising with private address.
Non-connectable advertising with BT_LE_ADV_OPT_USE_IDENTITY.
Connectable extended advertising.
Scannable extended advertising.
Non-connectable extended advertising with private address.
Non-connectable extended advertising with BT_LE_ADV_OPT_USE_IDENTITY.
Non-connectable extended advertising on coded PHY with private address.

memberdef.name: BT_LE_EXT_ADV_CODED_NCONN_IDENTITY
memberdef.initializer: BT_LE_ADV_PARAM(BT_LE_ADV_OPT_EXT_ADV | BT_LE_ADV_OPT_CODED | \ BT_LE_ADV_OPT_USE_IDENTITY, \ BT_GAP_ADV_FAST_INT_MIN_2, \ BT_GAP_ADV_FAST_INT_MAX_2, NULL)

briefdescription.para: Non-connectable extended advertising on coded PHY with BT_LE_ADV_OPT_USE_IDENTITY.
Helper to initialize extended advertising start parameters inline.
Helper to declare extended advertising start parameters inline.
Helper to declare periodic advertising parameters inline.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum periodic advertising interval, N * 0.625 milliseconds
Maximum periodic advertising interval, N * 0.625 milliseconds
Periodic advertising properties bitfield, see bt_le_adv_opt field.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare periodic advertising parameters inline.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum periodic advertising interval, N * 0.625 milliseconds
Maximum periodic advertising interval, N * 0.625 milliseconds
Periodic advertising properties bitfield, see bt_le_adv_opt field.

briefdescription.para: Initialize scan parameters.
Helper to declare scan parameters inline.
Helper macro to enable active scanning to discover new devices.
Helper macro to enable active scanning to discover new devices with window == interval.

detaileddescription.para: Continuous scanning should be used to maximize the chances of receiving advertising packets.

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to enable passive scanning to discover new devices.

detaileddescription.para: This macro should be used if information required for device identification (e.g., UUID) are known to be placed in Advertising Data.

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to enable passive scanning to discover new devices with window==interval.

detaileddescription.para: This macro should be used if information required for device identification (e.g., UUID) are known to be placed in Advertising Data.

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to enable active scanning to discover new devices.

detaileddescription.para: Include scanning on Coded PHY in addition to 1M PHY.

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to enable passive scanning to discover new devices.

detaileddescription.para: Include scanning on Coded PHY in addition to 1M PHY.
This macro should be used if information required for device identification (e.g., UUID) are known to be placed in Advertising Data.

briefdescription.para: Maximum size of Extended Inquiry Response.
Minimum BR/EDR scan interval (0x0012, 11.25 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Maximum BR/EDR scan interval (0x1000, 2.560 s, 0.625 ms units).
Minimum BR/EDR scan window (0x0011, 10.625 ms, 0.625 ms units).
Maximum BR/EDR scan window (0x1000, 2.560 s, 0.625 ms units).
Initialize BR Scan parameters.
Helper to declare BR/EDR page scan parameters inline.
Default page scan parameters for R0.

detaileddescription.para: Page scan interval and window are set to 1.280 seconds (0x0800 in 0.625 ms units). The scan type is set to standard.

briefdescription.para: Fast page scan parameters for R1.

detaileddescription.para: Page scan interval is set to 100 ms (0x00A0 in 0.625 ms units), and the page scan window is set to 10.625ms (0x0011 in 0.625 ms units). The scan type is set to interlaced.

briefdescription.para: Medium page scan parameters for R1.

detaileddescription.para: Page scan interval is set to 1.280 seconds (0x0800 in 0.625 ms units), and the page scan window is set to 10.625ms (0x0011 in 0.625 ms units). The scan type is set to interlaced.

briefdescription.para: Slow page scan parameters for R1.

detaileddescription.para: Page scan interval is set to 1.280 seconds (0x0800 in 0.625 ms units), and the page scan window is set to 10.625ms (0x0011 in 0.625 ms units). The scan type is set to standard.

briefdescription.para: Fast page scan parameters for R2.

detaileddescription.para: Page scan interval is set to 2.560 seconds (0x1000 in 0.625 ms units), and the page scan window is set to 10.625ms (0x0011 in 0.625 ms units). The scan type is set to interlaced.

briefdescription.para: Slow page scan parameters for R2.

detaileddescription.para: Page scan interval is set to 2.560 seconds (0x1000 in 0.625 ms units), and the page scan window is set to 10.625ms (0x0011 in 0.625 ms units). The scan type is set to standard.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare BR/EDR inquiry scan parameters inline.
Default inquiry scan parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Inquiry scan interval is set to 2.560 seconds (0x1000 in 0.625 ms units), and the inquiry scan window is set to 11.25 milliseconds (0x0012 in 0.625 ms units). The scan type is set to standard.

briefdescription.para: Generic Access Profile (GAP)

detaileddescription.para: The Generic Access Profile (GAP) defines fundamental Bluetooth operations, including device discovery, pairing, and connection management. Zephyr's GAP implementation supports both classic Bluetooth and Bluetooth Low Energy (LE) functionalities, enabling roles such as Broadcaster, Observer, Peripheral, and Central. These roles define the device's behavior in advertising, scanning, and establishing connections within Bluetooth networks.
